goal_spec_json.goal_statement: Sleep a minimum of 6 hours consistently every night

morning_briefing.morning_message:
Morning Ameena. 

You said you're at a 35 this morning — that makes complete sense given what happened overnight. Last night you got only 2.8 hours of sleep, which is critically short and over 2 hours less than your usual. Your body's recovery signals collapsed right alongside it: HRV plummeted to 48, showing severe physiological strain. What's especially telling is that this crash happened while you were exceptionally active yesterday — 14,600 steps and 157 minutes of vigorous movement. That mismatch means your system was pushed far beyond its recovery capacity. 

The data also shows your eating window was compressed, finishing around 5:27 AM, which likely overlapped with that fragmented sleep window. Despite feeling "Alert" yesterday, your body is running on fumes. 

Today is about one thing: damage control and gentle recovery. Your nervous system needs parasympathetic support, not more strain. 

The plan is to protect your evening fiercely and insert micro-breaks to lower the physiological load. 

Given the compounding strain, today's focus is on creating the calmest possible container for your system to begin repairing itself.

assumptions: User can adjust evening schedule for earlier wind-down
User has access to protein sources for nutrition reinforcement
User can take brief work breaks for recovery micro-actions
User is observing Ramadan with Suhoor/Iftar timing constraints
6am basketball practice is non-negotiable midweek commitment
hard_constraints: no additional high-intensity activity beyond scheduled practice
protect evening wind-down window starting by 7:00 PM
soft_constraints: caution with high energy expenditure given recent calorie/protein deficits
maintain earlier eating window (<1200 min) to support sleep
caution with late caffeine given sleep fragmentation risk
follow up on missing journal entries to clarify stressors
consider gentle movement only for recovery, not performance
day_constraints.high_stakes_reason: Critical physiological and cognitive risk: extremely low overall_score (35) with severe sleep debt (2.83h), critically low HRV (47.8), and sharp declines in overall_true_today (-12.32 slope_fast) and physio_proxy_score (-5.37 slope_fast). User is in acute recovery deficit with potential burnout risk.

generated_actions.title: Set a 7:00 PM alarm to start your wind-down ritual
generated_actions.rationale: LAST NIGHT's sleep was critically short (2.83h) and fragmented (efficiency 84.16%, wake ratio 15.84%). Given your severe sleep debt, today's 'sleep_consistency_recovery' priority, and the hard constraint to 'protect evening wind-down window starting by 7:00 PM', this external cue creates a protected buffer to aim for earlier sleep onset and prevent a repeat of the 2.83h duration.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did your 7:00 PM alarm help you initiate a longer, earlier wind-down tonight?
evaluation.success_definition: Sleep onset occurs before 8:00 PM (midnight-based minutes < 1200) AND user reports using the alarm to start a screen-free wind-down routine.

assumptions: You have a phone or alarm device
You can adjust your evening schedule
generated_actions.description: When your alarm goes off at 7:00 PM, stop all work, dim the lights, and begin a 60-minute screen-free transition: gentle tidying, reading, or light stretching. Aim to be in bed by 8:00 PM.

generated_actions.title: During your first work break, do 3 minutes of seated neck and shoulder rolls
generated_actions.rationale: Physiological recovery collapsed YESTERDAY: HRV plummeted to 47.8 (-12.66 slope_fast) and physio proxy score dropped to 27 (-5.37 slope_fast). This micro-action promotes parasympathetic activation without taxing your strained system, addressing the 'low_recovery' risk flag and the cross-domain signal 'high_activity_amid_recovery_collapse', while respecting the hard constraint of a 'tight schedule'.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did you complete 3 minutes of gentle neck and shoulder rolls during a work break today?
evaluation.success_definition: User reports completing the gentle movement and breathing routine during a work break.

assumptions: You can take a short work break
You can sit comfortably
generated_actions.description: When you take your first natural break from work, sit back, close your eyes, and slowly roll your neck and shoulders for 3 minutes. Focus on gentle movement and deep breathing, not stretching.

must_avoid: checking your phone during the break

generated_actions.title: When you feel 'Annoyed' or tension building, take a 2-minute visual grounding pause
generated_actions.rationale: YESTERDAY's stability was strained with emotions 'Annoyed' and 'Alert', and stress was low (34) but declining (-3.73 slope_fast). This implementation intention disrupts potential negative emotional spirals and leverages your reported 'Alert' state for a sensory reset, addressing the 'volatility' risk flag and the cross-domain signal 'subjective_alertness_amid_physiological_depletion'.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did you use the visual grounding technique when you felt annoyed or tense today?
evaluation.success_definition: User reports using the 'name 5 things' technique at least once when annoyance or tension arose.

assumptions: You can take brief pauses
You have access to a window or distant view
generated_actions.description: If the emotions 'Annoyed' (which you reported yesterday) or physical tension arise, immediately pause, look out a window or at a distant object, and silently name 5 things you can see. Breathe slowly.

must_avoid: continuing the stressful task without pause

generated_actions.title: Finish your Iftar meal by 5:30 PM tonight
generated_actions.rationale: YESTERDAY's eating window was extremely compressed (last meal at 5:27 AM, time_last_meal 327 min) and sleep was fragmented (wake ratio 15.84%). The cross-domain signal 'compressed_eating_with_sleep_disruption' suggests this pattern may contribute to sleep maintenance issues. Moving Iftar earlier directly addresses this and the soft constraint 'maintain earlier eating window (<1200 min) to support sleep', reinforcing your sleep goal domain.

evaluation.success_definition: Last meal occurs before 5:30 PM (midnight-based minutes < 1050), creating a longer buffer before your target early bedtime.

assumptions: You can adjust your Iftar timing slightly
generated_actions.description: Aim to finish your Ramadan Iftar meal by 5:30 PM local time. When you sit down to eat, note the time or set a gentle reminder to wrap up by this earlier cutoff.

previous_day_generated_actions.title: Start a 60-minute wind-down ritual at 6:30 PM tonight
previous_day_generated_actions.rationale: Yesterday's sleep was severely disrupted: duration 5.8h (below goal), efficiency 77.16% (critically low), wake ratio 22.62% (extremely high), and both efficiency and wake ratio are declining sharply (slope_fast -2.55 and +2.52). Today's priority is 'sleep_consistency_recovery'. An extremely early wind-down start creates a protected buffer to aim for earlier sleep onset and reduce fragmentation, directly addressing the 'sleep_debt' risk flag.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did your 6:30 PM wind-down ritual help you transition to bed earlier tonight?
evaluation.success_definition: Sleep onset occurs before 7:00 PM (midnight-based minutes < 1140) AND user reports initiating a screen-free wind-down routine.

assumptions: You can adjust your evening schedule
You have access to dim lighting
previous_day_generated_actions.description: When your evening begins, set a timer for 60 minutes of screen-free, low-light relaxation: dim your lights, avoid phones/TVs, and do gentle tidying, reading, or stretching. Aim to be in bed by 7:30 PM to maximize sleep opportunity.

previous_day_generated_actions.title: Take a 10-minute gentle walk after your basketball practice
previous_day_generated_actions.rationale: Physiological recovery is critically low (HRV 67.2, physio proxy 41) and sharply declining (HRV slope_fast -9.75). Yesterday's activity was exceptionally high (290 MVPA minutes, 14,842 steps). This gentle post-practice walk promotes parasympathetic activation without taxing your strained system, addressing the 'low_recovery' risk flag and the cross-domain signal 'high_activity_with_low_recovery', while respecting the soft constraint 'prioritize sleep over additional activity'.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did you take a 10-minute gentle walk after your basketball practice this morning?
evaluation.success_definition: User reports completing a gentle walk after their morning practice.

You can walk gently

You have access to a window or distant view

previous_day_briefing.morning_message:
Hey Ameena.

You're feeling fantastic this morning — a perfect 100 score with high energy. That's amazing, and it's the 6th day in a row you've felt this optimistic despite what your body is telling you. Let's look at the data. **Last night's sleep was rough:** you got only 5.8 hours with 77% efficiency, meaning you were awake nearly 23% of the time. That's a sharp decline from your usual pattern. Your body's recovery signals are in the red too: HRV dropped to 67.2, the lowest it's been, and your physiological proxy is at 41. That's your nervous system saying it's under strain.

Yesterday you were a powerhouse — 290 minutes of intense activity and nearly 15,000 steps. That's a huge rebound. But the data shows a clear cross‑domain signal: that massive activity volume is coinciding with critically low recovery. Your body is doing the work, but it's not getting the rest it needs to repair. Your focus (92) and coping (85) are incredibly strong, which explains why you feel so good mentally even while your physiology is struggling.

Today is about honoring that disconnect. The goal is to support your body's recovery so your high energy can be sustainable, not borrowed.

I've picked a few small moves designed to give your system the space it's asking for. You've got the mental firepower; today's about matching it with physical support.

evidence_quotes: Nothing really notable happened today. I am content with the day.

meal_descriptions.description: Quarter of a chicken quesadilla and a few tortilla chips and a 200 calorie protein bar with 20g of protein
Fried parmesean chicken breast with a side of salad and a side of fries
200 calorie protein bar with 20 grams of protein
